ddls:
CREATE TABLE "testing_data" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each testing data entry */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date when the testing data was reported */,
  "cumulative_total" INTEGER /* Cumulative total number of people tested up to that date */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country where the testing data was collected */,
  "units" TEXT /* Units in which the testing data is reported (e.g., people tested) */,
  "source_label" TEXT /* Label for the source of the testing data */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL for the source of the testing data */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the testing data */,
  "testing_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of testing performed */,
  "new_cases" INTEGER /* Number of new cases reported on the test date */,
  "new_deaths" INTEGER /* Number of new deaths reported on the test date */,
  "total_cases" INTEGER /* Total number of cases reported up to the test date */,
  "total_deaths" INTEGER /* Total number of deaths reported up to the test date */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testing_data_testing_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("testing_type_id") REFERENCES testing_types ("testing_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testing_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_testing_data_source_label FOREIGN KEY ("source_label") REFERENCES sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent where the country is located */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code for the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "testing_types" (
  "testing_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each testing type */,
  "testing_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the testing type (e.g., PCR, antibody) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the testing type */,
  "detection_method" TEXT /* Method used for detection (e.g., nasopharyngeal swab, blood test) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("testing_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_label" TEXT /* Label for the source of the testing data */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL for the source of the testing data */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the source */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the data provided by the source (e.g., CSV, JSON) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., health official, researcher, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the testing data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the testing data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the testing data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES testing_data ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version of the testing data */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the testing data */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the testing data */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of changes made in this version */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was updated */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES testing_data ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_criteria" TEXT /* Criteria used to generate the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the generated report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "news_articles" (
  "article_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each news article */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the news article */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the news article */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the news article source */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Date when the news article was published */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("article_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "glossary" (
  "term_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each term */,
  "term" TEXT /* Term related to COVID-19 testing */,
  "definition" TEXT /* Definition of the term */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("term_id")
)
